chunks.title: Instructions for the Burnt Offering
chunks.reason: This section contains a continuous legal instruction from Yahweh to Moses regarding the procedures for three types of burnt offerings: herd, flock, and birds.

chunks.title: Grain Offering of Fine Flour
chunks.reason: Establishes the basic requirements for a grain offering and the priest's portion.

chunks.title: Baked Grain Offerings
chunks.reason: Details specific preparations for grain offerings baked in an oven, on a griddle, or in a pan.

chunks.title: Seasoning and Leaven Rules
chunks.reason: Sets prohibitions against leaven and honey while mandating the use of salt.

chunks.title: First Fruits Offering
chunks.reason: Describes the specific procedure for bringing early ripened grain as a grain offering.

chunks.title: Peace Offering from the Herd
chunks.reason: Describes the procedure for a herd animal sacrifice.

chunks.title: Peace Offering from the Flock
chunks.reason: Describes the procedure for a sheep or lamb sacrifice.

chunks.title: Peace Offering from the Goat and Final Statute
chunks.reason: Describes the procedure for a goat sacrifice and concludes with dietary restrictions.

chunks.title: Sin Offering for the Anointed Priest
chunks.reason: Establishes the procedure for a priest's unintentional sin.

chunks.title: Sin Offering for the Congregation
chunks.reason: Details the ritual when the whole assembly sins unintentionally.

chunks.title: Sin Offering for a Leader
chunks.reason: Prescribes the goat sacrifice for a ruler's unintentional sin.

chunks.title: Sin Offering for Common People with Goat
chunks.reason: Outlines the offering for an individual from the common people.

chunks.title: Sin Offering for Common People with Lamb
chunks.reason: Specifies the alternative offering using a female lamb.

chunks.title: Sin Offering for Unintentional Sins
chunks.reason: A continuous legal section detailing four specific cases of unintentional sin and the corresponding tiered offerings based on the sinner's financial ability.

chunks.title: Guilt Offering for Holy Things
chunks.reason: A distinct legal unit introduced by a new divine address to Moses, covering the specific restitution required for sins against holy things and general unintentional violations.

chunks.title: Law of the Guilt Offering for Deceit
chunks.reason: A complete legal unit detailing restitution and sacrifice for specific sins against God and neighbor.

chunks.title: Law of the Burnt Offering
chunks.reason: A distinct legal section instructing priests on the maintenance and ritual of the burnt offering.

chunks.title: Law of the Grain Offering
chunks.reason: A complete legal unit covering the preparation, consumption, and holiness of the grain offering.

chunks.title: Law of the Priest's Grain Offering
chunks.reason: A specific legal instruction regarding the daily offering brought by the anointed priest.

chunks.title: Law of the Sin Offering
chunks.reason: A comprehensive legal section detailing the slaughter, consumption, and disposal of the sin offering.

chunks.title: Laws on Guilt and Grain Offerings
chunks.reason: Defines the procedure for the guilt offering and specifies which parts of grain offerings belong to the priests.

chunks.title: Laws on Peace Offerings
chunks.reason: Details the types of peace offerings, consumption rules for thanksgiving and votive sacrifices, and purity requirements.

chunks.title: Laws on Fat and Blood
chunks.reason: Prohibits eating fat and blood from specific animals with the penalty of being cut off.

chunks.title: Priestly Portions
chunks.reason: Establishes the specific portions of the peace offerings designated for Aaron and his sons as a perpetual statute.

chunks.title: Summary of Laws at Sinai
chunks.reason: Concludes the chapter by summarizing all preceding laws and anchoring them to the event at Mount Sinai.

chunks.title: Assembly and Preparation
chunks.reason: Moses gathers the congregation and announces God's command to consecrate Aaron.

chunks.title: Clothing the Priests
chunks.reason: Moses washes and clothes Aaron and his sons with priestly garments.

chunks.title: Sin Offering Ritual
chunks.reason: The bull is sacrificed for the sin offering and burned outside the camp.

chunks.title: Burnt Offering Ritual
chunks.reason: The first ram is sacrificed as a burnt offering for atonement.

chunks.title: Ram of Ordination
chunks.reason: The second ram is sacrificed with blood applied to priests and offerings waved.

chunks.title: Final Anointing
chunks.reason: Oil and blood are sprinkled to consecrate the priests and their garments.

chunks.title: Instructions for Seven Days
chunks.reason: Moses instructs the priests on eating the remainder and staying at the tent.

chunks.title: Moses commands the offerings
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of the eighth day event and Moses' instructions to Aaron and the people.

chunks.title: Aaron offers his own sacrifices
chunks.reason: Detailed account of Aaron performing the sin and burnt offerings for himself.

chunks.title: Aaron offers the people's sacrifices
chunks.reason: Continuation of the ritual focusing on the peace offerings and grain offering for the congregation.

chunks.title: Blessing and divine fire
chunks.reason: Conclusion of the ceremony with Aaron's blessing, Moses and Aaron entering the tent, and God's fire consuming the offering.

chunks.title: Death of Nadab and Abihu
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the priests' death and Moses' instructions to Aaron.

chunks.title: Wine Prohibition for Priests
chunks.reason: Divine command regarding wine and distinguishing holy from profane.

chunks.title: Priestly Food Portions
chunks.reason: Regulations on where and how priests must eat the grain and peace offerings.

chunks.title: Moses and Aaron Discuss Sin Offering
chunks.reason: Dialogue concerning the missing sin offering goat and Aaron's explanation.

chunks.title: Land Animals: Clean and Unclean
chunks.reason: Introduction to dietary laws for land animals with specific examples of unclean creatures.

chunks.title: Aquatic Animals: Clean and Unclean
chunks.reason: Criteria for edible water creatures based on fins and scales.

chunks.title: Birds and Flying Swarming Things
chunks.reason: Catalog of detestable birds followed by specific edible flying insects.

chunks.title: Purity Regulations for Swarming Things
chunks.reason: Rules regarding contact with carcasses of swarming things and their effect on vessels and food.

chunks.title: Unclean Food Animals
chunks.reason: Specific regulations for animals that die naturally.

chunks.title: Call to Holiness
chunks.reason: Summary of the swarming laws linked to the theological command for holiness.

chunks.title: Conclusion of the Law
chunks.reason: Formal summary statement defining the scope and purpose of the preceding laws.

chunks.title: Laws on Postpartum Purity
chunks.reason: A complete legal unit detailing the purification process for a woman after childbirth.

chunks.title: Initial Examination of Skin Infection
chunks.reason: Establishes the procedure for a priest to examine a swelling or scab and determine isolation periods.

chunks.title: Diagnosis of Chronic Leprosy
chunks.reason: Defines criteria for chronic leprosy and the specific conditions under which a person is pronounced clean or unclean.

chunks.title: Leprosy in Boils
chunks.reason: Outlines the examination process for a healed boil that develops a bright spot.

chunks.title: Leprosy in Burns
chunks.reason: Describes the protocol for examining a burn that develops a reddish-white bright spot.

chunks.title: Leprosy of the Head or Beard
chunks.reason: Details the examination and isolation process for a scaly infection on the head or beard.

chunks.title: Bright Spots and Baldness
chunks.reason: Distinguishes between eczema and leprosy in cases of faded white spots or infection on a bald head.

chunks.title: Appearance of the Leprous Man
chunks.reason: Specifies the clothing and behavior required for a person declared unclean with leprosy.

chunks.title: Leprosy in Garments and Leather
chunks.reason: Provides the complete procedure for examining, isolating, washing, or burning garments affected by a leprous mark.

chunks.title: Law for Cleansing a Leper
chunks.reason: A complete legal procedure detailing the ritual for cleansing a person with leprosy, including provisions for the wealthy and the poor.

chunks.title: Law for Cleansing a Leprous House
chunks.reason: A continuous legal section covering the diagnosis, isolation, and cleansing of a house with a leprous mark, concluding with a summary statement.

chunks.title: Law on Male Discharge
chunks.reason: Establishes the uncleanness of a male discharge and the required purification rituals.

chunks.title: Law on Seminal Emission
chunks.reason: Defines the uncleanness resulting from a seminal emission for men and women.

chunks.title: Law on Menstrual Impurity
chunks.reason: Details the uncleanness of a woman during her menstrual period and its transmission.

chunks.title: Law on Irregular Female Discharge
chunks.reason: Describes the regulations for a woman with a prolonged blood discharge.

chunks.title: Summary and Conclusion
chunks.reason: Summarizes the purpose of these laws to preserve the tabernacle and lists the covered cases.

chunks.title: The Day of Atonement Ritual
chunks.reason: Describes the complete sequence of actions performed by Aaron on the Day of Atonement.

chunks.title: Perpetual Statute for Atonement
chunks.reason: Establishes the annual ordinance and requirements for future priests.

chunks.title: Sacrifices in the Camp
chunks.reason: Yahweh commands that all slaughterings must be brought to the tent of meeting as peace offerings to avoid bloodguiltiness.

chunks.title: Prohibition on Eating Blood
chunks.reason: The text establishes the rule that both Israelites and sojourners must not eat blood because life is in the blood.

chunks.title: Ritual Purity for Unclean Meat
chunks.reason: Instructions are given for those who eat an animal that died naturally or was torn by beasts to wash and bathe.

chunks.title: Introduction to the Laws
chunks.reason: Yahweh introduces the statutes and judgments to Moses for Israel.

chunks.title: Incest Prohibitions
chunks.reason: A detailed catalog of relatives whose nakedness is to be uncovered.

chunks.title: Specific Sexual Laws
chunks.reason: Laws regarding menstrual impurity, neighbor's wife, Molech, males, and animals.

chunks.title: Consequences and Exhortation
chunks.reason: Explanation of the land's defilement and the warning to keep the statutes.

chunks.title: The Holiness Code
chunks.reason: A continuous collection of statutes and ethical commands given by Yahweh to Moses for the Israelites.

chunks.title: Judgments for Molech and Mediums
chunks.reason: Introduces the section with divine speech regarding specific capital offenses against God's holiness.

chunks.title: Call to Holiness
chunks.reason: A transitional command urging the people to set themselves apart based on God's nature.

chunks.title: Family and Sexual Purity Laws
chunks.reason: A detailed catalog of specific familial and sexual transgressions with their corresponding penalties.

chunks.title: Reasons for Separation
chunks.reason: Explains the theological basis for the laws by contrasting Israel with the nations and promising the land.

chunks.title: Judgment on Mediums
chunks.reason: Concludes the chapter with a specific legal ruling on mediums and spiritists.

chunks.title: Priests and Dead Relatives
chunks.reason: General laws for priests regarding mourning and marriage.

chunks.title: The High Priest's Restrictions
chunks.reason: Specific regulations for the high priest regarding mourning and wife selection.

chunks.title: Physical Defects in Priests
chunks.reason: Laws concerning physical defects preventing priests from offering sacrifices.

chunks.title: Moses Delivers the Message
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion stating Moses communicated the laws to Aaron and Israel.

chunks.title: Laws on Holy Gifts for Priests
chunks.reason: A continuous legal section addressing priestly purity and consumption of holy offerings.

chunks.title: Qualifications for Sacrificial Animals
chunks.reason: A distinct legal unit specifying physical requirements for burnt and peace offerings.

chunks.title: Laws on Young Animals and Thanksgiving
chunks.reason: A final legal section covering timing for young animals, slaughter rules, and thanksgiving offerings.

chunks.title: Introduction and Passover Feast
chunks.reason: Establishes the holy convocations framework and details the Passover and Unleavened Bread.

chunks.title: First Fruits and Weeks Feast
chunks.reason: Describes the wave sheaf offering, the counting to Pentecost, and the harvest gleaning laws.

chunks.title: Feast of Trumpets and Day of Atonement
chunks.reason: Outlines the seventh month festivals including the trumpet blast and the solemn day of atonement.

chunks.title: Feast of Booths and Conclusion
chunks.reason: Details the seven-day feast of tabernacles, its rituals, and concludes with Moses delivering the instructions.

chunks.title: Laws on Bread and Lamp
chunks.reason: Divine instructions regarding the perpetual lamp and showbread.

chunks.title: The Blasphemer Brought to Moses
chunks.reason: Narrative account of a specific incident leading to judicial inquiry.

chunks.title: Laws on Retribution and Blasphemy
chunks.reason: Divine decrees concerning the punishment for cursing God and striking others.

chunks.title: Execution of the Law
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion describing the fulfillment of the command.

chunks.title: The Sabbath Year for the Land
chunks.reason: Establishes the command for the land to rest every seventh year.

chunks.title: The Jubilee Year and Land Sales
chunks.reason: Describes the counting of years to the fiftieth year and regulations for buying and selling land.

chunks.title: Promise of Blessing in the Jubilee
chunks.reason: Addresses the concern of food supply during the fallow years with a divine promise.

chunks.title: Redemption of Land and Houses
chunks.reason: Details the rules for redeeming land sold due to poverty and specific laws regarding city houses.

chunks.title: Treatment of a Poor Brother
chunks.reason: Instructs on sustaining a poor relative without usury and treating him as a hired worker.

chunks.title: Acquisition of Foreign Slaves
chunks.reason: Distinguishes between Israelite servants and permanent slaves acquired from surrounding nations.

chunks.title: Redemption of a Sold Brother
chunks.reason: Explains the process for redeeming an Israelite who sold himself to a foreigner and concludes with the theological basis.

chunks.title: Statutes and Blessings
chunks.reason: A cohesive unit of laws regarding idols and sabbaths followed by the blessings for obedience.

chunks.title: Warnings of Punishment
chunks.reason: A progressive oracle detailing the curses and punishments that will follow disobedience.

chunks.title: Severe Discipline and Exile
chunks.reason: The climax of the curses describing extreme famine, exile, and psychological terror.

chunks.title: Promise of Restoration
chunks.reason: A speech outlining the conditions for confession and God's promise to remember His covenant.

chunks.title: Chapter Conclusion
chunks.reason: A concluding statement identifying the text as statutes given at Mount Sinai.

chunks.title: Valuation of Persons
chunks.reason: Introduction and specific silver valuations for men, women, and children based on age.

chunks.title: Valuation of Animals
chunks.reason: Rules for consecrating clean and unclean animals and their redemption.

chunks.title: Valuation of Houses and Fields
chunks.reason: Procedures for consecrating houses and fields of possession or purchase.

chunks.title: Firstborns, Devoted Items, and Tithes
chunks.reason: Specific laws regarding firstborn animals, devoted things, and the tithe of land and livestock.

chunks.title: Chapter Conclusion
chunks.reason: Final summary statement attributing the commandments to Moses at Sinai.
